column4: Title
Template Management UI v2: back-office page (dropdown select, combined list+edit, test-only upload, record nav)
Fix variance/Flex logic: tolerance handling, correct -flex schedule creation, date sync, auto-delete on unmatch
Cleanup: remove orphaned test schedules created during Rob Feb deposit testing
Reconciliation QoL: sync matched/unmatched views, auto-select bottom row, fix refresh states
Spec: draft standard reconciliation column layout (top/bottom, frozen columns, widths) and share with StrategixAI
Add Vendor Name + Distributor Name to deposit line item fields (reconciliation grids)
Revenue schedule details: show human Account ID (not UUID) + fix formatting
Multi-vendor upload: filter records by selected vendor/distributor; jump to first row; renumber record list
SME feature bugfix: save/trigger not firing; ensure product pop-up appears; notify Rob to test
Validate SME commission logic vs Rob’s SME commission report; identify gaps and required rule updates
SME commission guidance: show note in commission section when SME role selected (V–AK excluding AA)
Many-to-one/bundle matching: enforce commission-rate equality; if different, prompt to split schedule into individual schedules
AI experiment: create secondary concatenated “Other IDs” field (do not replace structured ID fields)
AI matching strategy update: hard-gate Vendor/Distributor/Dates; fuzzy-match other fields with confidence score
Many-to-one bundle conversion workflow: replace bundled product/schedule with individual products + schedule series
column5: When a deposit line item maps to multiple schedules with different commission rates, block the match and offer a remediation workflow that replaces the bundled product/schedule setup with separate products and schedule series built from the deposit data.
column6: Auto-validate rate conflict before match; plain-language prompt explains why bundle cannot be matched; on confirm, create separate opportunity products from deposit line data; create schedule series that mirrors original bundle timeline; remove/deactivate original bundled product + schedules; write audit trail.
column7: Expands MTG-0303-014. ASSUMED: original bundle removal should be soft-delete/audited, not hard delete.

column4: Commission-rate discrepancy prompt with optional apply-to-future update
column5: If received commission rate differs from expected rate during matching, show a prompt and let the user decide whether to update future schedules for that product instead of silently adjusting only the current match.
column6: Prompt shows expected vs received rate; works for over- and under-rate scenarios; user can keep current rate and flag/investigate or update future schedules in-flow; future schedule update does not require leaving reconciliation; current actuals still save correctly.
column7: ASSUMED: future-only change, not retroactive to reconciled schedules.

column4: Unmatch must fully reverse usage + commission adjustments, not just actuals
column5: Fix unmatch so it restores all pre-match values, including expected usage adjustment and commission adjustment, not only actual usage/commission.
column6: Unmatch clears actual usage, actual commission, expected usage adjustment, and commission adjustment; works for one-to-one and multi-item scenarios; rematch after unmatch behaves correctly; stale adjustments no longer distort confidence/matching.

column4: Live pre-match preview in revenue schedule grid with temporary values + cell highlighting
column5: As users select deposit lines and candidate schedules, temporarily preview resulting Actual Usage / Actual Commission in the bottom schedule grid before Match is clicked.
column6: Previewed values appear in-place without saving; deselecting rows reverts preview; changed cells are highlighted; temporary preview can include adjustment-related cells where relevant; interaction does not reload full page.
column7: Rob preferred replacing the displayed value in-cell vs showing a side-by-side delta.

column4: Matching modal UX cleanup: auto-validation, plain-language errors, clearer sections, use schedule numbers
column5: Clean up matching/variance pop-ups so users do not have to hunt for actions or interpret internal IDs.
column6: Validation runs automatically; no extra manual preview step for rate conflict; modal uses clear sections/dividers/contrast; copy is plain-language; previews show schedule numbers/names instead of raw IDs; buttons/actions are clearly labeled.
column7: Depends on Rob’s final wording for matching + variance modal actions.

column4: Frozen-column lock behavior v2: lock in modal, move locked columns left, configure top/bottom separately
column5: Implement the final frozen-column behavior in the column-selection modal.
column6: Users can mark columns locked via checkbox/lock icon; locked columns pin left and override normal order; users can still reorder columns; top and bottom tables can have different locked columns; preference persists per user.
column7: Expands MTG-0303-006 with final agreed behavior.

column4: Swap Match button to Unmatch in matched view
column5: In matched context, replace Match with Unmatch so the next available action is obvious.
column6: When viewing matched items, primary action reads Unmatch; user does not need to reveal a hidden second state; no duplicate/conflicting Match + Unmatch buttons for the same matched row; works for multi-item matched selections.
column7: This is a UX clarification/finalization of the earlier unmatch-button discussion.

column4: Fix revenue schedule row-update + percent formatting bugs in commission-rate displays
column5: Resolve the bad row-update behavior in revenue schedules and fix remaining percent-formatting issues where rates are rendering on a 0–1 scale instead of 0–100.
column6: Row updates reflect saved values immediately; commission-rate fields render consistently as percentages; SME scenarios display 8 vs 16 correctly; no stale row state after save/update.
column7: Transcript confirms both a row-update bug and a remaining percent-formatting issue.

column4: Purge obsolete reconciliation test schedules from prior sessions
column5: Delete stale test schedules and temporary overage artifacts so current reconciliation testing is clean.
column6: Remove the $29.90 and $2.40 schedules and other obsolete prior-session artifacts called out in meeting; preserve currently valid test schedules; document what was removed.
